description: Nigerian religious demographics — national and per-state where available.

national.conflict_note: Alternative Pew Research 2015 data: Muslim 50%, Christian 48.1%, other ~2%. Nigerian government does not publish official religion figures. 53.5/45.9 figures are from Wikipedia citing NBS/general sources.

sources.name: Pew Research 2015 — Nigeria's Divided Future
sources.note: Not yet fetched; cited by Wikipedia

geopolitical_zone_summary.note: General pattern based on public knowledge; not sourced from a single retrieved document. Needs NBS/Pew per-state data to verify.
geopolitical_zone_summary.NC: Mixed — northern LGAs predominantly Muslim, southern LGAs predominantly Christian
geopolitical_zone_summary.SW: Mixed — roughly 50/50 Muslim/Christian in Yoruba states

gaps.note: Per-state religion breakdown not retrieved. Nigeria's government does not publish official per-state religion figures. Best source: Pew Research 2010/2015 subnational estimates or academic surveys (Afrobarometer).
Year of 53.5/45.9 figure unclear from Wikipedia source
